g.text: LSN Tracking: The Connective Tissue (Steal/No-Force Policy)
Before a dirty page (LSN_page) is flushed to disk, the log up to that LSN must be flushed first.
Steal/No-Force minimizes I/O stalls but increases recovery complexity.

g.title: Buffer pool can evict dirty pages to disk before transaction commits.
Dirty pages must stay pinned in memory until EOT.
Commit does not require flushing modified pages to data files.
All modified pages must be synced to disk at commit time.

g.text: Required to roll back dirty data on disk from aborted Xacts
Required to replay committed changes missing from data files
The connective tissue linking Log to Data Pages

g.title: Buffer pool can evict dirty pages to disk before transaction commits.

Commit does not require flushing modified pages to data files.
